Event title: Gallery Performance: Celtic Art Across the Ages
description: Harpist and historian Nancy Hurrell will perform Irish music on an 1820s Egan harp and a modern Celtic harp. She will also share the history of the Celtic harp.

Event title: Hrishikesh Hirway, moderated by Min Jin Lee
description: Hirway is a singer/songwriter, podcast creator and composer. Prior to his work under his own name, he released four albums under the moniker The One AM Radio, as well as an EP with Moors, his project with Oscar-nominated Lakeith Stanfield. Hirway’s new album is “In the Last Hour of Light.”

Event title: Sh!t-Faced Shakespeare: Macbeth
description: Shakespeare’s tale of murder, ambition, bloodlust, and the supernatural. Bloody Macbeth and his murderous wife Lady M seize the Scottish throne, with one cast member who happens to be drunk.

Event title: David Wax Museum and Lowland Hum present The Golden Hours
description: Catch the unveiling of The Golden Hours, a new band formed by the members of indie-folk acts David Wax Museum and Lowland Hum. These musicians have been touring and recording for over 20 years, featured on “CBS Saturday Morning” and NPR’s “Tiny Desk.” The Golden Hours’ debut record is “Terra Nova.” Also performing on June 13.

Event title: Fresh Pond Day 2026
description: This 16th Annual Festival-style celebration of water, community and sustainability.There will be live music, an open house of the Water Treatment Plant with structured tour at 1 p.m., live animal exhibits, Touch-a-Truck, free popcorn and reusable water bottles and kids’ crafts and games.

Event title: Science Spotlights: Building a Planet from the Inside Out
Venue: Harvard Museum of Natural History, Classroom B
description: Harvard scientist Jack Sheehan explains how researchers recreate the extreme conditions of Earth’s interior in the lab to uncover how our planet formed and continues to evolve.

Event title: Fifth Annual Juneteenth Gathering
Venue: Longfellow House and the Washington’s Headquarters National Historic Site
description: Denise Washington, a descendant of Darby Vassall, who was enslaved at birth in Longfellow House but lived most of his life in freedom, has curated this day of poetry, music, historical displays, family activities and refreshments.

Event title: No Recombination Without Representation
description: Central Square Theater and the MIT Museum present a reenactment of a crowded city hall meeting in 1976 that considered the prospect of a new biohazard lab designed for groundbreaking recombinant DNA research.

Event title: Rye Barcott reads from “Courage Can Save Us: Ten Extraordinary Americans and the Fight for Our Future”
description: Marine Corps veteran and co-founder and CEO of With Honor, a group that works alongside the House’s bipartisan For Country Caucus and a bipartisan group of senators to pass critical legislation in national security, national service and veterans’ affairs, tells the story of veterans serving in Congress who have crossed party lines for the good of the nation. Marine Corps veteran and the U.S. representative for Massachusetts’s 6th congressional district Congressman Seth Moulton joins.

Event title: Navigating the Crossroads: When Art and Technology Intersects and Diverges
description: Join the MIT Open Documentary Lab and Cambridge Public Library for a conversation with Emmy Award–winning artist and director Michaela Holland on immersive storytelling in virtual reality.

Event title: Berklee Summer in the City: Solidago
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: Solidago (Ella Wheeler) is a Maryland-born bassist and songwriter who is influenced by legends such as Marvin Gaye, Stevie Wonder, Michael Jackson, D’Angelo, Erykah Badu and Corinne Bailey Rae.

Event title: Soccer Watch Party: Iraq vs. Norway
Location: Harvard Square, Church and Brattle streets.
description: Enjoy free food, face painting, soccer activities and live entertainment. This event is part of “Cambridge United: Where the World Comes Together,” a citywide series of free, outdoor soccer screenings and community celebrations throughout summer 2026.

Event title: Lunchtime concert: Kayla Blackburn
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: Kayla Blackburn is an audio engineer and indie-folk singer-songwriter. As a songwriter, Kayla draws inspiration from artists like Patty Griffin and Tracy Chapman.

Event title: Matt Shore Does Songs At You: A Musical Comedy Show
description: With topics ranging from dealing with anxiety, trying to pay student loans, protecting yourself from Boston’s wild turkeys, and more, he’s got the song you didn’t know you needed.

Event title: Live reggae from Roots Alley Collective featuring special guest Dion Knibb
description: In celebration of Juneteenth and presented in collaboration with MIT Sloan School of Management and MIT’s African, Black, American, Caribbean Employee Resource Group, enjoy live music plus delicious food truck options, lawn games and more.

Event title: Park Sounds: Juneteenth Celebration with Tim Hall
description: PARK SOUNDS 2026 brings free outdoor musical performances featuring local artists to the front lawn of the Cambridge Public Library Main Branch. Tim Hall is an award-winning musician, performance poet and producer from Detroit who performs warm neo soul, jazz, and hip hop influenced music. The events are all-ages. Bring a chair, food and loved ones to celebrate the summer.

Event title: Soccer Watch Party: Scotland vs. Morocco
Location: Inman Square, Cambridge St. between Springfield and Prospect streets.
description: Enjoy free food, face painting, soccer activities and live entertainment. This event is part of “Cambridge United: Where the World Comes Together,” a citywide series of free, outdoor soccer screenings and community celebrations throughout summer 2026.

Event title: Jack Grady performs
description: Jack Grady is a comedy magician whose chaotic, unpredictable style has earned him a cult following and millions of fans across the internet.

Event title: ¡Música! with Mariana Iranzi
description: A bilingual family concert in English and Spanish. Recommended for children of all ages and their caregivers.

Event title: Saturday Salsa night: La Diáspora Combo
description: Led by lead vocalist and multi-instrumentalist Angel Javier Camilo, La Diáspora Combo is an intergenerational collective of musicians from Latin America and the United States. Rooted in diasporic identity and community, the ensemble brings high-energy, dance-forward performances of salsa, merengue, and bachata.

Event title: Mount Auburn Summer Solstice 2026
description: Offerings of free and paid activities including mindfulness walks, yoga, a cyanotype making workshop, meditation, a poetry reading and community concert.

Event title: Summer Solstice Celebration 2026: Night at the Harvard Museums of Science & Culture
description: Enjoy live music and performers, play lawn games and make a flower crown. Ice cream, beverages and food will be available for purchase from food trucks.

Event title: Cambridge Cooks: Caribbean Heritage Month Edition with Chef Gaitskell
description: Join Chef Gaitskell Cleghorn Jr. as he prepares jerk chicken and mango salsa in celebration of Caribbean-American Heritage Month.

Event title: Bluegrass: Sam Reid & The Riot Act
description: Sam Reid and the Riot Act is an American roots and bluegrass band that is acoustic guitar driven.

Event title: Soccer Watch Party: England vs. Ghana
description: Enjoy free food, face painting, soccer activities and live entertainment. This event is part of “Cambridge United: Where the World Comes Together,” a citywide series of free, outdoor soccer screenings and community celebrations throughout summer 2026.

Event title: Berklee Summer in the City: The Apparatus Trio
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: The Apparatus Trio is a jazz fusion group redefining the edges of groove, improvisation and modern sonic textures.

Event title: Lunchtime concert: Rosa Joe Jacobs
Venue: Urban Park Roof Garden at Kendall Center
description: Rosa Joe Jacobs is a Brooklyn-bred, now Boston-based songwriter and musician. Her songs are drawn from folk traditions. Her debut is set to come out in 2027.

Event title: Astonished By Beauty: The Essential Practice of Natural History
description: Join naturalist Thomas Fleischner on his new book, “Astonished by Beauty,” a guide to reconnecting with nature through natural history.

Event title: Harvard Art Museums at Night
description: Gather with friends and mingle inside an Italian-inspired courtyard while taking in the smooth sounds from DJ C-Zone. Stop by the Materials Lab for a fun art-making activity and then drop in on one of the museum’s Spotlight Tours.

Event title: Cookbook Club and Community Potluck
Venue: Cambridge Public Library’s Central Square Branch
description: This month, we’re cooking from “In Bibi’s Kitchen: The Recipes and Stories of Grandmothers from the Eight African Countries that Touch the Indian Ocean” by Hawa Hassan. Bring a dish to share or just come to talk about your experience with this cookbook.
